name: Aberdeen Proving Ground
tagline: The Army's oldest active proving ground and a major research, testing, and acquisition hub in northeastern Maryland.
_note: Army research/test/acquisition hub (Harford County, NE Maryland; on the Chesapeake). CONUS full template (BAH MHA MD127 Aberdeen Proving Grounds; RentCast/SchoolDigger work). Heavy civilian/contractor workforce; most live off post. Tenants incl. CECOM, ARL, ATEC, DEVCOM. Medical: Kirk Army Health Clinic. Facts verified from official sources (home.army.mil/apg, Kirk AHC, DeCA/AAFES, Wikipedia for corroborated headline facts). Maryland is TRICARE East. Medium-confidence flags: base coords (approx), off-base ZIP 21001 (Aberdeen).

data.Location: Harford County, MD (Chesapeake Bay, ~30 mi NE of Baltimore)
data.Role: The Army's oldest active proving ground; research, testing, and acquisition
data.Major tenants: CECOM, Army Research Laboratory, ATEC, and DEVCOM elements

sections.title: Mission & Identity
data: Aberdeen Proving Ground is the Army's oldest active proving ground and a major center for research, testing, and acquisition. It hosts commands like CECOM, the Army Research Laboratory, ATEC, and DEVCOM elements, with a heavily civilian and contractor workforce.

On the Chesapeake Bay in Harford County, about 30 miles northeast of Baltimore, it functions more like a technology and engineering campus than an operational post, with most people living off post in the surrounding communities.

sections.title: A Tech-Hub Tour Between Baltimore and the Bay

data.subtitle: APG is a research and acquisition post with limited on-post housing, so the real question is which Harford County (or wider Baltimore-area) community fits your commute and schools.
intro: Because the workforce is largely civilian and contractor, most families live off post across Harford County and the northeastern Baltimore suburbs. The area offers a range of prices and well-regarded schools.
Where you live mostly comes down to commute and schools, with the bay and Baltimore both within easy reach.

recommendations.advice: Aberdeen, Bel Air, and Abingdon put you close to APG with a short commute and lots of amenities.
Bel Air and the broader Harford County area are popular with families for their schools.
Heading toward Havre de Grace or farther out can trade a longer commute for more home for the money.
On-post housing is limited; most live off post, so start your search in Harford County early.

sections.title: Gates & Access
data: Visitors without a DoD ID need a visitor pass and a REAL ID-compliant ID from the Visitor Control Center.
Gate hours and visitor requirements change, so confirm the current details on the official APG site before you go.

sections.title: On-Base Housing
data: APG has a limited amount of on-post family housing, so the great majority of personnel live off post across Harford County and the surrounding area.
Start with the housing office for the current on-post options and for help finding a place off post in the surrounding communities.

source.label: DoD 2026 BAH Rates, MHA MD127 (Aberdeen Proving Grounds)

sections.title: Schools
data: There is no DoDEA district here; children attend the local civilian public schools. Harford County Public Schools serves most military families, with neighboring districts depending on where you live.
Your assigned schools depend on your address, so confirm the district and campus for any neighborhood you are considering.

data.Care: Outpatient primary and specialty care on post; inpatient, emergency, and complex care via Baltimore-area network hospitals

intro: Whether to rent or buy is the biggest money decision of most PCS moves, and at APG the longer (often civilian) tours and stable Harford County market make buying a real option.
rent: Your tour is short (about two years or less) or your orders feel uncertain.
You want time to learn Harford County before committing.
You'd rather not gamble on resale timing when you PCS out.
Your BAH comfortably covers rent in the areas you'd consider.
buy: You expect to be here three or more years, common at a research hub.
You want to use your VA loan and build equity instead of paying rent.
The stable Harford County market fits your budget for a home you like.
You've found a neighborhood and commute you're confident about.
data.bottom_line: There is no universal right answer, but APG's longer tours and steady local market make buying worth a look. Run your BAH against the rent and home prices above, weigh your tour length, and talk to a lender and an agent who work with military families before you commit.

intro: The first month at a new duty station is mostly logistics. Knocking these out early makes everything else easier.

steps.title: Line up temporary lodging
steps.text: Book on-base lodging or a short-term rental before you arrive. You can claim Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) for a limited number of days while you house-hunt.

steps.title: In-process and update DEERS
steps.text: Report to your unit and finance, and make sure DEERS and ID cards are current for every family member so TRICARE and base access work.

steps.title: Start with the Housing Office
steps.text: On-post housing is limited; get the housing office's read on Harford County before you sign anything.

steps.title: Register your vehicle and sort access
steps.text: Handle base access and Maryland vehicle registration, and learn the visitor-pass process for guests. Bring your registration and insurance.

steps.title: Enroll the kids in school
steps.text: Contact Harford County Public Schools (or your local district) early and bring records and immunizations.

steps.title: Set up healthcare
steps.text: Update your TRICARE enrollment so you can be seen at Kirk Army Health Clinic or network providers.

data.q: What happens at Aberdeen Proving Ground?
data.a: It's the Army's oldest active proving ground and a major research, testing, and acquisition hub, home to commands like CECOM and the Army Research Laboratory. The workforce is largely civilian.
Mostly off post in Harford County. Aberdeen, Bel Air, and Abingdon are close to the gate; Bel Air is popular for schools.

data.q: Is it a good place to buy?
data.a: Often. Tours can be long or civilian and the Harford County market is stable, so buying is common.
APG is MHA MD127 (Aberdeen Proving Grounds). Use the calculator above for your grade.
